stages.logText: In the middle of a history lecture in Brightport, a student stood up during the teacher’s retelling of the Battle of Brightport, seemingly to contest the accuracy of the account, but was told to sit down.
At the end of the lecture, all of the students dispersed. I should find that student if I want to find out what they had to say.
Frederich, the teacher, said Eiden is misinformed and often objects to him, claiming he knows better.
According to Frederich, I can find Eiden hanging out with other students from Nor City, often at the bakery.
I asked Eiden what he had raised his hand to say, but he was hesitant to tell me.
I earned Eiden's trust with a bit of gold, a common practice in his birth city. It surely put him at ease.
He told me he had heard a different version of the retelling of the battle of Brightport, the monsters that emerged did not differentiate between feygard or Nor city in their carnage.
Eiden said he believes that is not the full story either, and he felt his uncle was hiding something when he was inquiring for an essay.
I found the cabin Eiden told me about. His uncle did not tell me his name, nor did he wish to speak to me. He only wanted to be left alone.
After much pestering, the Isolated man agreed to speak if I bring him some Charwood sap.
It seems that no matter what I tell the man, he is unlikely to trust me at the moment. This sounds like a matter for another time.
After I brought the sap, Heiter finally told me his name.
He said he received this land after his brother died in the Battle of Brightport. He had not witnessed it himself, but spoke with people who knew what happened.
Heiter laments his brother's death and wonders whether he died with any regrets. He told me to speak with the Doughe if I wish to learn what has been happening to the forest.

The Doughe, Rubiano, seeing as it was Heiter who directed me here, inferred the situation at hand. He had sent adventurers who reported that monsters similar to those from the battle many years ago have begun to appear in the depths of the decaying forest.
He requested the lord of Brightport to send a group of veteran adventurers from Feygard to help investigate further, but only one novice named Eckhart was sent.
It has been a while since Eckhart went off on his mission, and the Doughe is worried the worst has happened. He asked me if I could help locate Eckhart, but no more than that.

In a cave deep within the decayed forest, I found a structure filled not with the monsters I had heard of, but with vicious statues defending the ruins. Eckhart was further in, inside a relatively safer part of the caves.
He told me monster-repelling reagents had been applied at the entrances. Luckily, I arrived before his supply ran out.
Before we could conclude our conversation, a loud rumble was heard. Eckhart said the same thing had happened to him, and now we were stuck.
I went to investigate, and where the passage had been open before, a pool of lava now sat.

Eckhart said there are stairs in the eastern cave leading out somewhere, but a monster stands before them, so massive it has to hunch down to fit inside the cave. If I can make it outside through them, we could find an alternative way to leave.
Up on the mountain I found a group of fire creatures - spirits or whatever you can call them. Fire and lava are quite similar, they could be the cause of this.
The spirits are fickle and capricious. They speak our tongue then keep quiet, but behind them is an altar - perhaps I could appease them.

After the right amount of offerings, the fire spirit in the form of a person spoke to me. It said they were bound here by an offer they accepted long ago, but the offering I had made repaid their due part of the contract.

After I had dealt with the fire spirits to stop the flow of lava, Eckhart handed me his notes to deliver to the Doughe, and said he will continue deeper in, to find the cause for the worsening condition of the forest.
